title: melaleuca macrocarpa
intro: Melaleuca macrocarpa, also known as the Queensland tea tree, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the myrtle family (Myrtaceae) and being a close relative of the well-known melaleuca alternifolia. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which is rich in compounds like cineole and limonene, and is known for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Melaleuca macrocarpa have been used to promote health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, soothing skin irritations, and reducing stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of Melaleuca macrocarpa include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or taken orally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
